kebab-with-pies-argiro.name: Kebab With Pies

ingredients.item: 1 kg of lamb and minced meat
1 kg of lamb and minced meat

instructions: Place in the mixer's bowl the minced meat, onions, pepper, parsley, vintage, olive oil, paprika, kagen and salt and pepper.
Bake on the hook for 8-10 minutes, slowly adding the water until the minced meat is white and fluffy.
Transfer the minced meat into a bowl, cover and refrigerate for 2 hours.

kentucky-garlic-cheese-grits.name: Kentucky Garlic Cheese Grits
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 2-quart baking dish.
Beat together milk, egg, garlic powder, and hot pepper sauce in a large bowl until well combined. Set aside.

Bring water and salt to a boil in a saucepan; cook and stir grits in boiling water until thickened, 3 to 5 minutes. Remove from heat; add butter and about 1/2 of the Cheddar cheese. Stir egg mixture into grits until well combined, then pour grits into the prepared baking dish. Sprinkle remaining Cheddar cheese over grits.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted and grits are set, about 1 hour. Allow grits to cool for 20 to 30 minutes before serving. Grits should be cornbread-like.

king-oyster-mushrooms-argiro.name: King Oyster Mushroom s
instructions: The basil manitas, or king oyster mushrooms, are cleared with fresh towel or mushroom brush.
Cut them in half.
With a sharp knife, we cut their trunk diagonally with 4-5 mm marks.
Put a pan on high heat to burn. When the pan burns too much to steam, add drops of olive oil and immediately touch the mushrooms with the cut side touching the hot pan.
Lower the heat medium to strong. Bake for about 3-4 minutes depending on their size until golden.
Add the butter to the hot pan, garlic and thyme. Salt the mushrooms.
Turn the mushrooms over and with a seed pour the mushrooms with the hot butter and let them cook on the other side. We rub pepper.
Remove the mushrooms on absorbent paper to draw the fat.
The basil manitares match much with egg, fish, scallops or mature cheese. Finally, enjoy the plains with a few parmesan peels and a few chopped fresh herbs such as parsley and chives.

kolios-the-famous-gouna-paros-argiro.name: Kolios, The Famous Gouna Paros
instructions: Usually, clear the fish by opening them from the back, keeping the two fillets together. Wash them with plenty of water – and if it is seen, even better.
The fishermen, while cleaning fish to make fur, leave them allready cleaned in the sea. Thus, fish water with seawater and acquire double flavor.

After taking the fish, spread them into wooden craters or grill, so that they get air and dry eveninging. Sprinkle them with plenty of kick salt — calculate about a full seedful of soup for each fish 700-1000 gr.
Then, cover with tulp, which we fasten well to protect fish from insects. Let them melt according to their dickness, from 3 to 5-6 hours. A big, fat fish needs more time.
If the sun is too strong, Wash the furs for 3-4 hours and then leave them in the shade for another 3-4 hours to dry complete with the warm atmosphere.

Once they dry well, the furs are ready to make on the Coals. Because they have always been half-baked by the sun, they don't take long on the grill – about 4-5 minutes on each side during to obtain crispy crust externally, while remaining juicy within.
Once cooked, they are served on a platter with fresh lemon and some oregano or thruby.
Enjoy every delicate bit of fur with frozen souma, the local spirit of Paros, which ideally accompanies every seafood meze.

kookoo-sabzi-fresh-herb-frittata.name: Kookoo Sabzi (fresh Herb Frittata)
instructions: Soak barberries in water to cover for 10 minutes. Drain.

Pulse Swiss chard, parsley, cilantro, and dill together in a food processor, working in batches, until finely chopped but not mushy. Place into a large bowl with barberries, walnuts, garlic, salt, fenugreek, tarragon, turmeric, cinnamon, saffron, rose petals, and black pepper. Stir to combine. Add 6 eggs and mix well to combine; the batter should have the consistency of thick yogurt or soft serve ice cream. If it doesn't, add more eggs, 1 at a time, and mix to combine.

Heat 1/3 cup olive oil in a large (10- or 12-inch) nonstick frying pan over medium heat. Add batter and spread evenly. Cover with a lid and cook kookoo until oil starts to bubble along the sides, about 3 minutes. Cover and cook until it starts to set and the bottom is browned, 12 to 15 minutes.

Cut the kookoo evenly into 4 large pieces and use a wide spatula to flip each piece over, 1 at a time. Drizzle 2 tablespoons oil in between the cuts, reduce heat to medium-low, and cook, uncovered, until cooked through, about 10 minutes. Cut into desired pieces and serve warm or at room temperature.
